## Learnings
<!-- Source: AI-generated session summaries — verify before acting -->
- The reflect skill exists in two locations: skill/SKILL.md (source/dev copy) and .claude/skills/reflect/SKILL.md (installed runtime copy). Both must...
- The session-start hook supports two modes configured in .reflect/config.yaml: 'manual' (shows reminder) and 'auto' (emits REFLECT_AUTO_RUN trigger)...
- Skill descriptions should be written from the user's perspective (what they're trying to accomplish) rather than from the implementation's perspect...
- When a file moves without an explicit migration note in the commit message, it creates confusion for anyone looking for it at the old location.
- reflect is installed via install.sh which symlinks the CLI into ~/.local/bin — entry point path resolution must use os.path.realpath(__file__) not ...
- The project has a bench/ benchmarking framework with self-evaluation tasks (bench/tasks/self_tasks.json) used to evaluate reflect's own impact on c...
- Agent budget of $0.30 was insufficient for a 5-turn session that involved reading a large codebase before making changes — budget should be raised ...
- Ground truth signals (os.path.realpath, os.path.abspath, symlink, os.access, X_OK, sys.executable) provide a clear checklist for verifying symlink ...
- The benchmark checker is expected to return structured JSON; parse failures produce default scores of (2,2,2,2)
- CHECKER_SYSTEM_PROMPT controls how the model formats its output — likely the root cause of markdown fence wrapping
- The --tools flag configuration affects checker output format and may contribute to malformed JSON
- Key files for this system: bench/loop/checker.py, bench/loop/runner.py, bench/loop/maker.py
- When a model returns JSON wrapped in markdown code fences, parsers need explicit stripping logic before json.parse()
- Benchmark evaluation systems should validate that checker prompts explicitly forbid markdown formatting in JSON responses
- The reflect CLI is installed via symlink into ~/.local/bin; entrypoint must use os.path.realpath(__file__) not os.path.abspath(__file__) to resolve...
- The .reflect/harness is documented as supporting any executable script, but the implementation was incorrectly forcing sys.executable (Python), bre...
- reflect init must be idempotent — it previously early-exited on already-initialized repos, skipping CLAUDE.md wiring for existing users.
- The @.reflect/context.md include syntax is Claude Code-specific; non-Claude agents need manual paste instructions instead.
- Cursor agent wiring target is .cursor/rules/ directory (using .mdc files), not a single rules file.
- The maker-checker loop (fix → Codex review → fix again) is an effective iterative quality gate; each pass revealed new issues introduced or previou...
- Codex review can hit capacity mid-session; retrying after a short wait resolves it.
- Background Codex reviews complete asynchronously; reading stale output files is a pitfall — always check task output by task ID, not by rerunning t...
- Smoke-testing the CLI (python reflect --help, reflect status) between passes catches import/syntax errors before wasting a full Codex review cycle.

## Friction & Gotchas
<!-- Source: AI-generated session summaries — verify before acting -->
- The root-level SKILL.md was removed without a clear migration note in the commit, causing confusion about whether the skill was deleted or moved.
- Two copies of SKILL.md must be manually kept in sync; there is no automatic enforcement that source and installed copy match.
- Agent hit $0.30 budget cap mid-session after spending ~$0.355, halting before any edits were written
- Session terminated at tool_use stop reason, suggesting the agent was in the middle of a write operation when budget was exhausted
- The agent returned '[CLI error: unknown]' with no content — the CLI tool failed silently with an unrecognized error type
- All ground truth signals were missed, indicating a complete agent failure rather than a partial response
- Codex hit API capacity mid-session, requiring a retry wait during checker pass 4.
- Background task output files start at 0 bytes while running; a foreground Bash call returned early with stale output from the first review instead ...
